qa_pairs.question: What is an important consideration when choosing a version control tool?
qa_pairs.answer: An important consideration when choosing a version control tool is where you will host your repository.

qa_pairs.question: When is a local repository sufficient for a project?
qa_pairs.answer: A local repository is sufficient if you and your collaborators are always working on the same single machine.

qa_pairs.question: When would an internet-accessible repository be helpful?
qa_pairs.answer: An internet-accessible repository would be helpful if you are working across multiple machines or with collaborators working on different machines.

qa_pairs.question: What are the benefits of using an internet-accessible repository?
qa_pairs.answer: An internet-accessible repository allows you to easily synchronize your code between machines and provides additional safety for your code by being distributed.

qa_pairs.question: What are the ways to host a repository?
qa_pairs.answer: There are two main ways to host a repository: by setting it up yourself on your own server or by using one of the available online services.

qa_pairs.question: Can you provide examples of tools for self-hosting a repository on your own server?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, examples of tools for self-hosting a repository on your own server include SVN, Git, GitLab, and GitBucket.

qa_pairs.question: Name some online services that host repositories.
qa_pairs.answer: Bitbucket, GitHub, GitLab, and SourceForge are examples of online services that host repositories.

qa_pairs.question: What is an advantage of using online repository hosting services?
qa_pairs.answer: An advantage of using online repository hosting services is that they are hosted on the service providers' servers and do not require you to have a server that is always accessible.

qa_pairs.question: Is there a resource available for learning the basics of version control with Git?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, there is a very short video demonstrating the basics of version control with Git mentioned in the 'See also' section.
